interfaces.name: Habit CRUD API
interfaces.description: RESTful interface for creating, reading, updating, and deleting user habits
interfaces.contract: Supports POST /habits, GET /habits/{id}, PUT /habits/{id}, DELETE /habits/{id}; requires user authentication; returns habit details including name, schedule, and completion status

interfaces.name: Reminder Scheduler
interfaces.description: Service to register, update, and cancel scheduled reminders based on user-defined times
interfaces.contract: Accepts habit ID, user ID, and schedule (cron-like); triggers Reminder Delivery Service at scheduled time; handles timezone-aware scheduling

interfaces.name: Reminder Delivery Service
interfaces.description: Orchestrates delivery of reminders via configured messaging platforms
interfaces.contract: Receives trigger from Reminder Scheduler; routes message to SMS, Telegram, or WhatsApp based on user preference; implements retry logic on failure

interfaces.name: Messaging Platform Adapters
interfaces.description: Wrapper services for external messaging APIs (SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp)
interfaces.contract: Abstracts vendor-specific APIs; standardizes message format and error handling; enforces rate limit compliance

interfaces.name: Progress Chart Data API
interfaces.description: Provides time-series habit completion data for chart rendering
interfaces.contract: GET /habits/{id}/progress?range=30d; returns daily completion status; supports aggregation by week/month

interfaces.name: Streak Calculation Engine
interfaces.description: Listens to habit completion events and computes current streaks
interfaces.contract: Consumes 'HabitCompleted' event; updates streak count; emits 'StreakUpdated' and 'StreakThresholdReached' events

interfaces.name: User Authentication Service
interfaces.description: Handles user login, session management, and identity verification
interfaces.contract: OAuth2 or JWT-based; integrates with frontend and all backend services

interfaces.name: Frontend Web Application
interfaces.description: UI components and state management for habit tracking
interfaces.contract: React-based; consumes Habit CRUD API, Progress Chart Data API, and Streak Calculation Engine outputs

dataContracts.notes: Includes name, description, reminder schedule, creation date, and associated goal (optional)
Stores user ID, authentication tokens, preferred messaging channels, and contact identifiers (phone, Telegram ID, WhatsApp number)
Daily record of whether a habit was completed; used for progress charts and streak calculation
Maps habits to delivery times and channels; supports recurring schedules
Tracks current consecutive days of habit completion; resets on missed day
Logs all reminder delivery attempts, successes, failures; used for monitoring and retry

scalability.expectedLoad: 10K concurrent users, 100K daily active users, average 3 habits per user, peak reminder bursts at 8-9 AM and 6-7 PM
bottlenecks: Messaging platform API rate limits during peak hours
Streak recalculation under high write load
Chart data queries over large date ranges
mitigations: Implement message queuing (e.g., SQS) for reminder delivery with backpressure
Use caching (Redis) for streak and progress data
Distribute scheduling with worker pools and time-sharded jobs

securityBaseline.authMethod: JWT with refresh tokens, secured over HTTPS
securityBaseline.dataClassification: PII (phone numbers, messaging IDs), user behavior data (habit completions)
threatVectors: Unauthorized access to habit and streak data
SMS spoofing or phishing via reminder messages
Rate limiting abuse on public endpoints
Data exfiltration via insecure chart data API
mitigations: Encrypt PII at rest and in transit
Rotate API keys for external messaging services
Validate and sanitize all user inputs
Enforce strict CORS and rate limiting on APIs
Audit log all data access and modification

adrs.title: Adopt RESTful APIs for internal service communication
adrs.context: FR-01, FR-02, FR-08 require reliable CRUD operations; NFR-04 requires web accessibility; internal dependencies include multiple services needing interoperability
adrs.decision: Use REST over HTTP with JSON payloads for all internal service APIs
adrs.consequences: Simplifies frontend integration and debugging; may require additional effort to enforce consistency across services

adrs.title: Implement event-driven streak calculation
adrs.context: FR-04 and FR-10 require real-time streak updates; AC-04 and AC-05 demand immediate feedback on habit completion
adrs.decision: Use event sourcing pattern: emit 'HabitCompleted' event on completion; consume by Streak Calculation Engine
adrs.consequences: Enables reliable streak tracking and notifications; adds complexity with message broker dependency

adrs.title: Abstract external messaging APIs behind adapter layer
adrs.context: FR-05, FR-06, FR-07 and NFR-01 require reliable delivery across SMS, Telegram, WhatsApp; externalDependencies list high-risk integrations with rate limits
adrs.decision: Build adapter services per platform to normalize message sending, error handling, and retry logic
adrs.consequences: Improves resilience and testability; increases operational overhead for monitoring each adapter

adrs.title: Use PostgreSQL as single source of truth for core entities
adrs.context: FR-01, FR-02, FR-08 require durable storage; internalDependencies include data storage and retrieval needs
adrs.decision: Store Habit, User, HabitCompletion, Streak, and ReminderSchedule in PostgreSQL with proper indexing
adrs.consequences: Ensures ACID compliance and referential integrity; may require read replicas for scaling analytics queries

gaps: No user authentication requirements specified in contract
Lack of defined data retention policies
No explicit performance SLAs for API response times
Missing compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR, TCPA for SMS)
No disaster recovery or backup strategy documented
Undefined fallback behavior for failed message deliveries
No internationalization or timezone handling details
Unspecified cost constraints for external API usage
